{{神社 |名称 =相馬神社 |画像 = |所在地 =北海道札幌市豊平区平岸2条18丁目1番1号 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|1|14|N|141|21|53|E|scale:20000}} |祭神 =天之御中主大神 |社格 = |創建 ={{和暦|1871}} |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =9月5日 |神事 = }}
'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。
== 沿革 == *{{和暦|1871}}3月 [[水沢藩]]出身の入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *{{和暦|1902}}1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地の仮神殿に奉斎。 *{{和暦|1909}}[[11月26日]] 豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *{{和暦|1916}}[[8月1日]] 平岸村849番地（現在地）に移転。 *{{和暦|1928}}[[10月9日]] 現社殿の改築遷座祭。
== 祭神 == *[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）
== 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄南北線]][[澄川駅]]から徒歩1分程度。
== 関連項目 ==
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:豊平区]]

'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。
== 沿革 == *{{和暦|1871}}3月 [[水沢藩]]出身の入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *{{和暦|1902}}1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地の仮神殿に奉斎。 *{{和暦|1909}}[[11月26日]] 豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *{{和暦|1916}}[[8月1日]] 平岸村849番地（現在地）に移転。 *{{和暦|1928}}[[10月9日]] 現社殿の改築遷座祭。
== 祭神 == *[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）
== 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄南北線]][[澄川駅]]から徒歩1分程度。
== 御神木 == *直径121cm、樹高10m *樹齢300年を超えているこの御神木は、[[1968年]][[8月5日]]に札幌市の保存樹に指定。

{{神社 |名称 =相馬神社 |画像 = |所在地 =北海道札幌市豊平区平岸2条18丁目1番1号 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|43|1|14|N|141|21|53|E|scale:20000}} |祭神 =天之御中主大神 |社格 = |創建 ={{和暦|1871}} |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =9月5日 |神事 = }}
'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。
== 沿革 == *{{和暦|1871}}3月 [[水沢藩]]出身の入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *{{和暦|1902}}1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地（現[[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]6条7丁目、豊平会館）の仮神殿に奉斎。 *{{和暦|1908}}[[10月27日]] 神社創立を認可され公認神社となる。 *{{和暦|1909}}[[11月26日]] 本殿落成遷座祭斎行。豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *{{和暦|1916}}[[8月1日]] 平岸村山の上437番地の現在地（天神山）に移転。 *{{和暦|1928}}[[10月9日]] [[昭和天皇]]御即位記念として社殿をご造営。本殿御造営改築遷座祭斎行。（旧社殿） *{{和暦|1953}}[[2月26日]] [[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人を設立。 *{{和暦|1965}}[[10月30日]] 社務所新築。 *{{和暦|1976}}[[3月1日]] 付近一帯が、天神山緑地保全地区に指定される。 *{{和暦|2007}}[[8月25日]] 創立百周年記念事業御社殿改修工事竣工本殿遷座祭斎行。（現社殿） *{{和暦|2008}}[[6月24日]] 創立百周年記念大祭並びに記念祝賀会斎行。
== 祭神 == *[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）
== 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄南北線]][[澄川駅]]から徒歩7分程度。

'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。
== 沿革 == *{{和暦|1871}}3月 [[水沢藩]]出身の入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *{{和暦|1902}}1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡 (福島県)|相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地（現[[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]6条7丁目、豊平会館）の仮神殿に奉斎。 *{{和暦|1908}}[[10月27日]] 神社創立を認可され公認神社となる。 *{{和暦|1909}}[[11月26日]] 本殿落成遷座祭斎行。豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *{{和暦|1916}}[[8月1日]] 平岸村山の上437番地の現在地（天神山）に移転。 *{{和暦|1928}}[[10月9日]] [[昭和天皇]]御即位記念として社殿をご造営。本殿御造営改築遷座祭斎行。（旧社殿） *{{和暦|1953}}[[2月26日]] [[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人を設立。 *{{和暦|1965}}[[10月30日]] 社務所新築。 *{{和暦|1976}}[[3月1日]] 付近一帯が、天神山緑地保全地区に指定される。 *{{和暦|2007}}[[8月25日]] 創立百周年記念事業御社殿改修工事竣工本殿遷座祭斎行。（現社殿） *{{和暦|2008}}[[6月24日]] 創立百周年記念大祭並びに記念祝賀会斎行。
== 祭神 == *[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）
== 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄南北線]][[澄川駅]]から徒歩7分程度。
== 御神木 == *直径121cm、樹高10m *樹齢300年を超えているこの御神木は、[[1968年]][[8月5日]]に札幌市の保存樹に指定。
== 関連項目 == *[[天神山 (北海道)]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:豊平区]]

'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。
== 沿革 == *{{和暦|1871}}3月 [[仙台藩]]領[[水沢市|水沢]]からの入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *{{和暦|1902}}1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡 (福島県)|相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地（現[[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]6条7丁目、豊平会館）の仮神殿に奉斎。 *{{和暦|1908}}[[10月27日]] 神社創立を認可され公認神社となる。 *{{和暦|1909}}[[11月26日]] 本殿落成遷座祭斎行。豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *{{和暦|1916}}[[8月1日]] 平岸村山の上437番地の現在地（天神山）に移転。 *{{和暦|1928}}[[10月9日]] [[昭和天皇]]御即位記念として社殿をご造営。本殿御造営改築遷座祭斎行。（旧社殿） *{{和暦|1953}}[[2月26日]] [[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人を設立。 *{{和暦|1965}}[[10月30日]] 社務所新築。 *{{和暦|1976}}[[3月1日]] 付近一帯が、天神山緑地保全地区に指定される。 *{{和暦|2007}}[[8月25日]] 創立百周年記念事業御社殿改修工事竣工本殿遷座祭斎行。（現社殿） *{{和暦|2008}}[[6月24日]] 創立百周年記念大祭並びに記念祝賀会斎行。

'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は無格社。
== 沿革 == *[[1871年]]（明治4年）3月 [[仙台藩]]領[[水沢市|水沢]]からの入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *[[1902年]]（明治35年）1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡 (福島県)|相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地（現[[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]6条7丁目、豊平会館）の仮神殿に奉斎。 *[[1908年]]（明治41年）[[10月27日]] 神社創立を認可され公認神社となる。 *[[1909年]]（明治42年）[[11月26日]] 本殿落成遷座祭斎行。豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[8月1日]] 平岸村山の上437番地の現在地（天神山）に移転。 *[[1928年]]（昭和3年）[[10月9日]] [[昭和天皇]]御即位記念として社殿をご造営。本殿御造営改築遷座祭斎行。（旧社殿） *[[1953年]]（昭和28年）[[2月26日]] [[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人を設立。 *[[1965年]]（昭和40年）[[10月30日]] 社務所新築。 *[[1976年]]（昭和51年）[[3月1日]] 付近一帯が、天神山緑地保全地区に指定される。 *[[2007年]]（平成19年）[[8月25日]] 創立百周年記念事業御社殿改修工事竣工本殿遷座祭斎行。（現社殿） *[[2008年]]（平成20年）[[6月24日]] 創立百周年記念大祭並びに記念祝賀会斎行。

== 沿革 == *[[1871年]]（明治4年）3月 [[仙台藩]]領[[水沢市|水沢]]からの入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *[[1902年]]（明治35年）1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地（現[[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]6条7丁目、豊平会館）の仮神殿に奉斎。 *[[1908年]]（明治41年）10月27日 神社創立を認可され公認神社となる。 *[[1909年]]（明治42年）11月26日 本殿落成遷座祭斎行。豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *[[1916年]]（大正5年）8月1日 平岸村山の上437番地の現在地（[[天神山 (北海道)|天神山]]）に移転。 *[[1928年]]（昭和3年）10月9日 [[昭和天皇]]御即位記念として社殿をご造営。本殿御造営改築遷座祭斎行（旧社殿）。 *[[1953年]]（昭和28年）2月26日 [[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人を設立。 *[[1965年]]（昭和40年）10月30日 社務所新築。 *[[1976年]]（昭和51年）3月1日 付近一帯が、天神山緑地保全地区に指定される。 *[[2007年]]（平成19年）8月25日 創立百周年記念事業御社殿改修工事竣工本殿遷座祭斎行。（現社殿） *[[2008年]]（平成20年）6月24日 創立百周年記念大祭並びに記念祝賀会斎行。
== 祭神 == *[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）
== 御神木 == [[クリ|シバグリ]]の大樹で、樹直径121cm、樹高10m。推定樹齢は300年以上<ref>『札幌秘境100選』</ref>。
[[1968年]]8月5日に札幌市の保存樹に指定された。
== 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄南北線]][[澄川駅]]から徒歩7分程度。 == ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|御神木 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|「馬頭観世音菩薩」碑 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|馬魂碑 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|平岸開村五拾年記念碑 </gallery> == 脚注 == <references /> == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Soma-jinja of Sapporo}} *「73 相馬神社のご神木（豊平区平岸）」、[[青木由直]]編著『札幌秘境100選』マップショップ、2006年10月。ISBN 4-9903282-0-5

{{神社 |名称 =相馬神社 |画像 =[[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px|center]] |所在地 =北海道札幌市豊平区平岸2条18丁目1番1号 | 緯度度 = 43 | 緯度分 = 1 | 緯度秒 = 13.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 141 |経度分 = 21 | 経度秒 = 53.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 =Sapporo2 |祭神 =天之御中主大神 |社格 =無格社 |創建 =[[1871年]]（明治4年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =9月5日 |神事 = }} '''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は無格社。
== 沿革 == *[[1871年]]（明治4年）3月 [[仙台藩]]領[[水沢市|水沢]]からの入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 *[[1902年]]（明治35年）1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地（現[[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]6条7丁目、豊平会館）の仮神殿に奉斎。 *[[1908年]]（明治41年）10月27日 神社創立を認可され公認神社となる。 *[[1909年]]（明治42年）11月26日 本殿落成遷座祭斎行。豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 *[[1916年]]（大正5年）8月1日 平岸村山の上437番地の現在地（[[天神山 (北海道)|天神山]]）に移転。 *[[1928年]]（昭和3年）10月9日 [[昭和天皇]]御即位記念として社殿をご造営。本殿御造営改築遷座祭斎行（旧社殿）。 *[[1953年]]（昭和28年）2月26日 [[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人を設立。 *[[1965年]]（昭和40年）10月30日 社務所新築。 *[[1976年]]（昭和51年）3月1日 付近一帯が、天神山緑地保全地区に指定される。 *[[2007年]]（平成19年）8月25日 創立百周年記念事業御社殿改修工事竣工本殿遷座祭斎行。（現社殿） *[[2008年]]（平成20年）6月24日 創立百周年記念大祭並びに記念祝賀会斎行。
== 祭神 == *[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）
== 御神木 == [[クリ|シバグリ]]の大樹で、樹直径121cm、樹高10m。推定樹齢は300年以上<ref>『札幌秘境100選』</ref>。
[[1968年]]8月5日に札幌市の保存樹に指定された。
== 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄南北線]][[澄川駅]]から徒歩7分程度。 == ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|御神木 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|「馬頭観世音菩薩」碑 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|馬魂碑 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|平岸開村五拾年記念碑 </gallery> == 脚注 == <references /> == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Soma-jinja of Sapporo}} *「73 相馬神社のご神木（豊平区平岸）」、[[青木由直]]編著『札幌秘境100選』マップショップ、2006年10月。ISBN 4-9903282-0-5 == 関連項目 == * [[平岸天満宮]] * [[天神山 (北海道)]] * [[柏木神社 (恵庭市)|柏木神社]]（[[相馬神社 (恵庭市)|相馬神社]]） {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧無格社]] [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=北海道}}

{{神社 |名称 =相馬神社 |画像 =[[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px|center]] |所在地 =北海道札幌市豊平区平岸2条18丁目1番1号 | 緯度度 = 43 | 緯度分 = 1 | 緯度秒 = 13.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 141 |経度分 = 21 | 経度秒 = 53.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 =Sapporo2 |祭神 =天之御中主大神 |社格 =無格社 |創建 =[[1871年]]（明治4年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =9月5日 |神事 = }} '''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[平岸 (札幌市)|平岸]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は無格社。
== 沿革 == * [[1871年]]（明治4年）3月 [[仙台藩]]領[[水沢市|水沢]]からの入植者により、現在地にて[[北海道神宮|札幌神社]]の遥拝所として祭事を執行。 * [[1902年]]（明治35年）1月 [[福島県]][[相馬郡]][[南相馬市|太田村]]に鎮座する[[相馬太田神社]]より御分霊を奉遷し、[[豊平町 (北海道)|豊平町]]5番地（現[[豊平 (札幌市)|豊平]]6条7丁目、豊平まちづくりセンター）の仮神殿に奉斎。 * [[1908年]]（明治41年）10月27日 神社創立を認可され公認神社となる。 * [[1909年]]（明治42年）11月26日 本殿落成遷座祭斎行。豊平町平岸村47番地に鎮座。 * [[1916年]]（大正5年）8月1日 平岸村山の上437番地の現在地（[[天神山 (北海道)|天神山]]）に移転。 * [[1928年]]（昭和3年）10月9日 [[昭和天皇]]御即位記念として社殿をご造営。本殿御造営改築遷座祭斎行（旧社殿）。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年）2月26日 [[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人を設立。 * [[1965年]]（昭和40年）10月30日 社務所新築。 * [[1976年]]（昭和51年）3月1日 付近一帯が、天神山緑地保全地区に指定される。 * [[2007年]]（平成19年）8月25日 創立百周年記念事業御社殿改修工事竣工本殿遷座祭斎行。（現社殿） * [[2008年]]（平成20年）6月24日 創立百周年記念大祭並びに記念祝賀会斎行。
== 祭神 == *[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）
== 御神木 == [[クリ|シバグリ]]の大樹で、樹直径121cm、樹高10m。推定樹齢は300年以上<ref>『札幌秘境100選』</ref>。
[[1968年]]8月5日に札幌市の保存樹に指定された。
== 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄南北線]][[澄川駅]]から徒歩7分程度。 == ギャラリー == <gallery> File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|御神木 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|「馬頭観世音菩薩」碑 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|馬魂碑 File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|平岸開村五拾年記念碑 </gallery> == 脚注 == <references /> == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Soma-jinja of Sapporo}} *「73 相馬神社のご神木（豊平区平岸）」、[[青木由直]]編著『札幌秘境100選』マップショップ、2006年10月。ISBN 4-9903282-0-5 == 関連項目 == * [[平岸天満宮]] * [[天神山 (北海道)]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=北海道}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧無格社]] [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sōma Shrine |Image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |Founded = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Rank System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|遥拝所|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == Deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large {{ill|Shibaguri|ja|クリ}} chestnut tree with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Sōma shrines]] [[Category:Ungraded shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[ja:相馬神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sōma Shrine |Image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]

It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Rank System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|遥拝所|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == Deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large {{ill|Shibaguri|ja|クリ}} chestnut tree with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Sōma shrines]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Ungraded shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[ja:%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9C%AD%E5%B9%8C%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:相馬神社]]

It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Rank System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == Deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ungraded shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[ja:%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9C%AD%E5%B9%8C%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:相馬神社]]

It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Rank System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == Deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ungraded shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[ja:%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9C%AD%E5%B9%8C%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:相馬神社]]

It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Rank System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == Deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ungraded shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9C%AD%E5%B9%8C%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:相馬神社]]

It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ungraded shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9C%AD%E5%B9%8C%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:相馬神社]]

It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == Deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Sōma shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Ungraded shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[ja:%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9C%AD%E5%B9%8C%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:相馬神社]]

It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōma Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōma Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Souma Jinja Shrine Sapporo.jpg|240px]]
Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also! == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōma Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Hiragishi, Sapporo|ja|平岸 (札幌市)}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} |Latitude degrees = 43 |Latitude minutes = 1 |Latitude seconds = 13.6 |N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 141 |Longitude minutes = 21 |Longitude seconds = 53.2 |E/W = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Sapporo2 |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} |Shrine rank = Ungraded shrine |consecration_year = 1871 (Meiji 4) |Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alternate name = |Festival = September 5 |Ceremonies = }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Toyohira Ward, Sapporo|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, specifically in the Hiragishi district. It holds the historical rank of an ungraded shrine under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == * In March 1871 (Meiji 4), settlers from {{ill|Mizusawa, Iwate|ja|水沢市}}, formerly part of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|ja|仙台藩}}, established the shrine as a {{ill|yōhaijo|ja|遥拝所}} for {{ill|Hokkaido Shrine|ja|北海道神宮}}, conducting rituals at the current location. * In January 1902 (Meiji 35), a divided spirit of the deity from {{ill|Sōma Ōta Shrine|ja|相馬太田神社}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} was enshrined in a temporary hall at Toyohira Town (now Hiragishi 6-jō 7-chōme). * In October 1908 (Meiji 41), the shrine received official recognition as a public Shinto shrine. * In November 1909 (Meiji 42), the main hall was completed, and the shrine was relocated to its current location on {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山}}. * In 1928 (Shōwa 3), a new shrine building was constructed in commemoration of the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor's Enthronement|ja|昭和天皇即位}}. * In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the shrine was incorporated as a religious entity under {{ill|Jinja Honchō|ja|神社本庁}}. * In 2007 (Heisei 19), a major renovation was completed in celebration of the shrine's 100th anniversary. == deity == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神}} == Sacred Tree == The shrine's sacred tree is a large [[:en:Castanea_crenata|chestnut tree]] with a trunk diameter of 121 cm and a height of 10 meters, estimated to be over 300 years old青木由直編著『札幌秘境100選』. It was designated as a protected tree by Sapporo City on August 5, 1968. == Access == * Approximately a 7-minute walk from {{ill|Sumikawa Station|ja|澄川駅}} on the Sapporo Municipal Subway's Namboku Line. == Gallery == File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - Divine Tree.JPG|Sacred Tree File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to Hayagriva.JPG|Monument to Hayagriva File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to horses' spirits.JPG|Horse Spirit Monument File:Soma Shrine of Sapporo - monument to the 50th anniversary of Hiragishi.JPG|50th Anniversary Monument for Hiragishi Settlement == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Hiragishi Tenmangū|ja|平岸天満宮}} * {{ill|Tenjinyama, Hokkaido|ja|天神山 (北海道)}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E7%9B%B8%E9%A6%AC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Official site - Hokkaido Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Prefecture-stub|pref=Hokkaido}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōma Shrine
